{"id":"project-LICHEN-jnwe","title":"Unbounded Vec<String> growth for error messages","description":"## Location
rust/lichen-apps/src/bin/hetero_node.rs:25

## Description
The `errors: Vec<String>` field grows without bound. If the node experiences persistent errors (e.g., radio interference causing repeated RX/TX failures), error messages accumulate indefinitely. While the duration_s parameter provides some bound, a long-running node with high error rates could accumulate significant memory.

## Failure Scenario
Node runs for duration_s=86400 (24 hours) in a high-interference environment where 50% of TX/RX operations fail. With ~8640 TX attempts and ~43200 RX attempts, the errors Vec could accumulate ~25000+ String allocations, each containing debug format output of error types. Memory usage grows monotonically without cap.

## Category

new_value: Replaced unbounded errors: Vec<String> with error_count: u32 counter (only len() was used). Updated struct, new(), TX/RX error paths, and metrics JSON. Cargo check/clippy/test/fmt all pass. 3 codereview passes per workflow rule (no findings).
